# nested package: a new file in the child subtree is the child's drift, not the parent's
exec git init
exec git config user.email t@example.com
exec git config user.name t
exec git config commit.gpgsign false

# commit 1: parent (pkg) + child (pkg/child), both in sync
exec git add pkg/SPEC.md pkg/parent.go pkg/child/SPEC.md pkg/child/child.go
exec git commit -m init

# commit 2: add pkg/child/extra.go. Child drifts; parent must NOT count it.
cp extra.go.tmpl pkg/child/extra.go
exec git add pkg/child/extra.go
exec git commit -m edit

! psy check
stdout 'pkg: OK'
stdout 'pkg/child: NG'
stdout 'extra\.go \(added\)'
stdout 'summary: 1 drift'

-- pkg/parent.go --
package pkg
-- extra.go.tmpl --
package child
-- pkg/SPEC.md --
---
package: pkg
---

# 文件

- `parent.go` — p
-- pkg/child/child.go --
package child
-- pkg/child/SPEC.md --
---
package: pkg/child
---

# 文件

- `child.go` — c
